title: Treasury and IRS to Provide Guidance to Religious Organizations
summary: The U.S. Department of the Treasury and IRS announced plans to issue guidance clarifying the application of the Johnson Amendment to religious organizations, responding to recent litigation in National Religious Broadcasters v. Bessent. The guidance will address whether certain communications made within religious services constitute prohibited political campaign intervention under current tax law.
key_points: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent stated the guidance will provide clear, administrable standards for houses of worship
The guidance will address how the law applies to communications made within the context of religious services

The announcement references the National Religious Broadcasters v. Bessent case where the IRS proposed that bona fide communications internal to a house of worship do not constitute prohibited political campaign intervention
The federal district court dismissed the case for lack of jurisdiction before considering the proposed resolution
The guidance release date will be determined later in 2026
why_it_matters: This announcement concerns tax guidance for religious organizations regarding the Johnson Amendment (political campaign intervention by tax-exempt organizations). It has no relevance to market structure or macro economic catalysts. No connection to financial markets, interest rates, monetary policy, fiscal policy, economic indicators, or market operations.
